about.title: About
about.subtitle: A community center dedicated to serving our neighbors with dignity, respect, and care.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.p1: La Reunión exists to strengthen our community by providing essential services, fostering meaningful connections, and supporting individuals and families in times of need. We believe that every person deserves to be treated with dignity and respect.
mission.p2: As a faith-based organization, we are guided by our values of compassion, service, and community. However, we serve everyone regardless of their background, beliefs, or circumstances.

dignity.title: Dignity
dignity.description: We believe every person has inherent worth and deserves to be treated with respect.

community.title: Community
community.description: We foster connections and build relationships that strengthen our neighborhood.

service.title: Service
service.description: We are committed to meeting the real needs of our community with compassion and care.

partnership.title: Partnership
partnership.description: We work collaboratively with other organizations and community members to maximize our impact.

location.title: Our Location
location.subtitle: Located in the heart of Granbury, Texas, we serve our community from our welcoming community center on Maplewood Street.
location.description: We're conveniently located in Oak Trail Shores. Our center serves as a welcoming space for all community members seeking support, connection, and resources.

story.title: Our Story
story.p1: La Reunión has a rich history rooted in serving the uninsured and underserved communities of Hood County, Texas. Our story begins with Ruth's Place Outreach Center, which was originally part of the larger Ruth's Place organization.
story.p2: Initially located in Oak Trail Shores, Ruth's Place provided both medical and non-medical services to the community. In 2019, the medical clinic (now known as Ken Hill Health Center) moved to central Granbury, while the Outreach Center remained in Oak Trail Shores, continuing to focus on essential non-medical programs.
story.p3: These vital programs included a food pantry, after-school activities, and ESL classes, all designed to support and strengthen our community. The Outreach Center became a cornerstone of Oak Trail Shores, providing essential services to families and individuals in need.
story.p4: Recently, Ruth's Place partnered with First Christian Church of Granbury, which will take over the Outreach Center's functions. This transition ensures the continuation of these vital services in our community while marking the beginning of a new chapter.
story.p5: Now, Ruth's Place Outreach Center is becoming La Reunión - a community center dedicated to serving our neighbors with dignity, respect, and care. We continue the legacy of providing essential social services, food assistance, after-school programs, and community activities that make our community stronger.
story.p6: Today, La Reunión stands as a beacon of hope and support in our community, building on the foundation of service established by Ruth's Place while creating new opportunities to serve, connect, and strengthen the bonds that make us a stronger, more resilient community.

opportunities.title: Volunteer Opportunities
opportunities.subtitle: We have various volunteer positions available to match your interests, skills, and schedule.

foodPantry.title: Angelita 'Helen' Best Food Pantry Support
foodPantry.description: Help organize and distribute food items from the Tarrant Area Food Bank, assist with client check-ins, and maintain pantry organization.

afterSchool.title: After-School Program Assistant
afterSchool.description: Support children with homework, lead educational activities, and provide supervision during program hours.
afterSchool.time: Monday through Friday, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM (School Year)

grounds.title: Grounds Maintenance Team
grounds.description: Help maintain our community center grounds through mowing, weeding, power washing, and landscaping.
grounds.time: Flexible scheduling, typically 2-4 hours per week

admin.title: Administrative Support
admin.description: Assist with office tasks, data entry, phone calls, and general administrative duties.
admin.skills: Organization, computer skills, attention to detail

events.title: Event Planning & Support
events.description: Help plan and coordinate community events, fundraisers, and special programs.

teen.title: Teen Program Support
teen.description: Support teen programs by providing healthy snacks, helping with activities, and creating a welcoming environment.
teen.time: Varies by program, typically 2-3 hours per event

special.title: Special Projects
special.description: Contribute your unique skills and talents to help us better serve our community.

getStarted.title: How to Get Started
getStarted.subtitle: Ready to make a difference? Here's how you can begin volunteering with La Reunión.

step1.title: Contact Us
step1.description: Send us an email at info@la-reunion.org expressing your interest in volunteering. Tell us about your interests, skills, and availability.

step2.title: Schedule a Meeting
step2.description: We'll arrange a time to meet with you, discuss opportunities, and answer any questions you might have about volunteering with us.

step3.title: Begin Volunteering
step3.description: Once we find the right fit, you'll receive orientation and training, then begin making a difference in our community!

location.title: Where We're Located
location.subtitle: Join us at our community center in Oak Trail Shores, Texas, where all our volunteer opportunities are based.

aboutDonation.title: About Your Donation
aboutDonation.description: We are currently in a transitional period as ownership of the nonprofit center transfers to its new entity. During this time, all donations will go to the designated fund at First Christian Church of Granbury, which can only be spent to support La Reunión's operations, programs and services. Your donation will continue to directly benefit our community center and its mission.
